pattern: Post-order DFS: prune each child first, then drop the current node when it is a leaf holding zero, so subtrees free of any one collapse from the bottom up.
hints: Decide a node's fate only after both of its children have already been pruned.
Recurse into the children and reattach the pruned results; a returned null severs that branch entirely.
After pruning both sides, if the node has become a childless leaf whose value is zero, return null so it too is removed.

edge_inputs.description: right spine where a zero subtree is dropped
balanced tree pruned to a thin right path
single zero node prunes to an empty tree
internal node retained only because a deep descendant holds a one

stress.description: random nearly-complete binary tree of zeros and ones in BFS wire form
stress.note: BFS level-order array with occasional nulls produces varied prunable shapes
